gazette: Official Gazette dated September 20, 2024, and numbered 32668
title: The Communiqué Amending the Communiqué on the Importation of Certain Electric Vehicles (Import: 2024/22) Has Been Published
bodyHtml:
The Communiqué Amending the Communiqué on the Importation of Certain Electric Vehicles (Import: 2024/22) has been published. As is known, according to the communiqué numbered 2024/22, in the case of electric vehicles under tariff position 8703.80, which are not of European Union origin or from countries with which Free Trade Agreements have been signed, and which are subject to the Free Circulation Regime, a "Permit Certificate" issued by the Ministry of Industry and Technology or an authorized institution or organization is required by customs administrations during the registration of customs declarations.

With the recent amendment, the list of goods covered by the Communiqué has been expanded to include hybrid motor vehicles designed for carrying goods.

The title of the Communiqué on the Importation of Certain Electric Vehicles (Import: 2024/22) has been changed to "The Communiqué on the Importation of Certain Electric and Externally Rechargeable Hybrid Vehicles (Import: 2024/22)."

The purpose of the Communiqué has been amended as "the regulation of the importation of electric or externally rechargeable hybrid vehicles subject to type approval."

The list of goods covered by the Communiqué has been redefined as follows:

follows:

HS CodeDescription of Goods
8703.60.10.00.00New vehicles
8703.70.00.00.00Other vehicles (equipped with both compression-ignition internal combustion piston engine (diesel or semi-diesel) and an electric motor providing propulsion) (charged by an external power source)
8703.80.10.00.21Vehicles equipped with a non-propulsion internal combustion engine
8703.80.10.00.29Others
8703.80.10.00.39Others
8704.41.31.00.00New vehicles (only externally rechargeable hybrids)
8704.41.91.00.00New vehicles (only externally rechargeable hybrids)
8704.42.91.00.00New vehicles (only externally rechargeable hybrids)
8704.43.91.00.00New vehicles (only externally rechargeable hybrids)
8704.51.31.00.00New vehicles (only externally rechargeable hybrids)
8704.51.91.00.00New vehicles (only externally rechargeable hybrids)
8704.52.91.00.00New vehicles (only externally rechargeable hybrids)
8704.60.00.00.00Others (only powered by an electric motor)
8704.90.00.00.00Others (only externally rechargeable hybrids)


In order to obtain a permit certificate:

The importer must have established at least 20 authorized service stations in 7 geographical regions, certified by the Turkish Standards Institute (TSE), for motor vehicles in the M and N classes as defined in the Highway Traffic Regulation published in the Official Gazette dated July 18, 1997, No. 23053 (M class vehicles including those in L6 and L7 classes equipped with M class vehicle equipment), according to TS 12047 and TSE K 646 standards, and for L class motor vehicles according to TS 11921 standard.

Individuals responsible for the maintenance and repair of electric or externally rechargeable hybrid vehicles must have a qualification certificate issued exclusively for the maintenance and repair of electric or externally rechargeable hybrid vehicles by TSE or the Vocational Qualification Authority.

For the import of electric or externally rechargeable hybrid vehicles that are not subject to type approval, a letter of exemption/out-of-scope issued by TSE will be required by customs administrations.

The amendments will come into effect on October 20.
